title: Spinach Mushroom Salad
description: A refreshing and savory spinach and mushroom salad, enhanced with hard-boiled eggs, crisp bacon, and a tangy dressing. Perfect as a light meal or a flavorful side dish.
instructions:
1. Remove stems from spinach.
2. Tear into bite size pieces. Combine spinach, mushrooms, eggs, bacon and green onions in a large bowl.
3. Combine oil, vinegar, sugar and seasonings in a jar.
4. Cover tightly and shake vigorously.
5. Top spinach mixture with dressing just before serving.

title: Hungarian Stuffed Cabbage - Mom'S Way
description: Hearty Hungarian stuffed cabbage rolls, filled with a mixture of ground beef and pork, barley, and spices, simmered in a rich tomato and sauerkraut sauce. A comforting and traditional meal.
instructions:
1. Core cabbage and place in enough boiling water to cover.
2. With a fork in one hand and a knife in the other, keep cutting off the leaves as they wilt.
3. Drain and trim center vein of each leaf. Set aside.

title: Cream Cheese Blintzes
description: Sweet and creamy blintzes made with flattened white bread, filled with a rich cream cheese mixture, then dipped in butter and rolled in cinnamon sugar. A delightful dessert or breakfast treat.
instructions:
1. Roll bread flat with a rolling pin.
2. Mix cream cheese, 1/2 c. sugar and egg yolks together.
3. Spread evenly over the bread and rollup lengthwise.
4. Dip in melted butter and roll in cinnamon sugar.
5. Cool in fridge for 20 minutes and then cut into thirds. This can be frozen at this point and stored up to 4 months.
6. Bake 350° for 20 minutes or bubbly.

title: All-In-One Meat Loaf Dinner(This Dish Is Similar In Flavor To Stuffed Cabbage.)
description: A comforting all-in-one meatloaf dinner, featuring a savory lean ground beef loaf baked with cabbage, onions, and tomatoes, reminiscent of stuffed cabbage flavors. Garnished with Swiss cheese and fresh parsley.
instructions:
1. Preheat oven to 350°.
2. Spread the oil in a 14 x 11 x 3-inch baking pan. Add the cabbage, onion, tomatoes and sugar. Add salt and pepper and mix well.
3. Cover with foil and bake for 45 minutes, stirring occasionally.

title: Pierogie Casserole
description: A simple and comforting pierogie casserole made with boiled noodles, potatoes, and onions. This dish offers a quick and easy way to enjoy pierogi-inspired flavors.
instructions:
1. Boil noodles and drain.
2. (If using lasagne, lay flat until ready to use.)

title: Blintz Bubble Ring
description: An innovative blintz bubble ring, featuring cream cheese balls wrapped in refrigerator biscuits, rolled in cinnamon sugar, and baked with butter and pecans. A sweet and indulgent treat perfect for any occasion.
instructions:
1. Cut cream cheese into 20 pieces.
2. Roll into balls.
3. Roll each biscuit into a 3-inch circle.
4. Combine sugar and cinnamon.
5. Place 1 cheese ball and 1 teaspoon cinnamon mixture in the center of each biscuit; pinch dough to seal.
6. Pour butter or margarine into the bottom of a 5-cup ring mold.
7. Sprinkle half of the nuts and half of remaining cinnamon mixture into mold.
8. Top with half of the rolls, placing seam side up.
9. Repeat layers.
10. Bake at 350° for 20 minutes, or until brown.
11. Cool in pan for 5 minutes.
12. Invert onto serving plate.

title: Pasta Pierogi
description: A straightforward pasta pierogi dish combining boiled potatoes and noodles with sautéed onions, then topped with melted American cheese. A comforting and easy-to-prepare meal.
instructions:
1. Boil and drain potatoes.
2. Cook noodles and drain.
3. Saute onion in oleo until tender.
4. Add potatoes and noodles and stir until coated with onion mixture.
5. Top with cheese.
6. When cheese is melted, serve.

title: Russian Dressing For 50
description: A classic Russian dressing suitable for large gatherings, featuring a blend of mayonnaise, eggs, peppers, pimento, relish, chili sauce, lemon, onion, and garlic. Perfect for salads or as a dip.
instructions: 1. Mix and store in refrigerator.

title: Houston Baked Beans
description: Hearty Houston baked beans, enriched with a mix of pork and kidney beans, brown sugar, and a crispy bacon topping. A perfect side dish for any barbecue or comforting meal.
instructions:
1. Saute bacon and onion.
2. Combine all ingredients and bake uncovered at 350° for one hour.

title: Edna'S Jam Cake
description: A delightful jam cake featuring four layers of moist cake, infused with spices like nutmeg, allspice, cinnamon, and cloves, and enriched with raisins, coconut, and blackberry jam. A truly indulgent dessert.
instructions: 1. Bake 4 layers in 350° oven until done.

title: Butterscotch Cream Cake
description: A decadent butterscotch cream cake, prepared from a mix but elevated with milk, eggs, butterscotch dessert mix, and vanilla. This rich cake promises a delightful butterscotch flavor perfect for any celebration.
instructions:
1. Prepare cake mix adding milk, eggs, the 1/2 cup butterscotch dessert mix and the 1 teaspoon vanilla extract.
2. Mix and bake as directed on package in three greased and wax paper lined, 9-inch layer pans.
3. Cool 10 minutes; remove from pans and cool completely.

title: Mini-Blinies
description: Mini-blinies crafted from flattened white bread, filled with a sweet cream cheese and egg yolk mixture, then rolled, dipped in butter, and coated in cinnamon sugar. A charming and easy alternative to traditional blini.
instructions:
1. Trim crust from bread and flatten with a rolling pin.
2. Mix cream cheese, egg yolks and 1/2 cup sugar.
3. Spread with cream cheese mixture and roll each slice of bread like a jelly roll.
4. Dip in melted butter and roll in mixture of 1/2 cup sugar and 1 teaspoon cinnamon.
5. Place on a cookie sheet and freeze.

title: Pickled Polish Sausage
description: Zesty pickled Polish sausage, cut into pieces and simmered before being submerged in a tangy brine of vinegar, sugar, salt, peppercorns, allspice, and onion. A flavorful and hearty appetizer or snack.
instructions:
1. Cut sausage into 2 to 3-inch pieces.
2. Simmer 1 hour and drain and rinse.
3. Combine remaining ingredients and bring to a boil. Simmer 10 minutes.
4. Let cool.
5. Pour this mixture over cooled sausage and some chopped onion in a large glass jar.
6. Keep in refrigerator.

title: Casey'S Delicious Honey Rye Bread
description: Casey's delicious honey rye bread, featuring a comforting blend of milk, honey, salt, caraway seeds, rye flour, and wheat flour. This homemade bread offers a rich flavor and satisfying texture, perfect for any meal.
instructions:
1. Mix together milk, honey, salt and caraway seed; cool.
2. When at room temperature, add yeast.
3. Add rye flour and 1 cup wheat flour; beat thoroughly!
4. Add 2 1/2 cups wheat flour; mix to make a stiff dough.
5. Turn out of mixing bowl onto a lightly floured board or counter.
6. Knead until smooth and elastic.
7. Form a ball and put into a greased plastic bowl (I use Pam spray).
8. Turn the ball around in the bowl evenly, allowing the coating to seal the bottom of the ball.
9. Then place the bottom of this ball of dough, now coated, facing up in bowl.
10. Cover bowl with a piece of plastic wrap.
11. Store in a warm place (85°) until doubled.
12. Takes about 45 minutes. Remove the dough from the bowl onto a lightly floured board or counter and divide into two equal pieces.
13. Do not punch the air out of the dough.
14. Shape into loaves and place in 2 greased (or Pam sprayed) 9 x 5 x 3-inch pans.
15. Cover with plastic wrap.

title: Russian Spice Tea
description: A quick and easy Russian spice tea mix, combining powdered orange drink, instant tea, sugar, cinnamon, and cloves. Simply add to hot or cold water for a a warming and aromatic beverage.
instructions:
1. Mix all together well.
2. Store in tight jar.
3. To mix, add 2 to 3 teaspoons to 1 cup of hot or cold water.
4. For larger quantities, add 1 cup of mixture to 10 cups water.

title: Lazy Pierogi
description: A comforting 'lazy pierogi' dish, combining egg noodles and cooked cabbage with browned hamburger, flavored with lemon juice, onions, and salt pork. A hearty and flavorful one-pan meal.
instructions:
1. Cook noodles; drain and set aside.
2. Core and cook cabbage (quartered).
3. Add lemon juice to water while cooking 25 minutes until done.
4. Drain and dice cabbage.
5. Brown hamburger; drain and set aside in bowl.
6. In same pan, place salt pork.
7. On low heat, cook until most of drippings are out.
8. Discard pork rind.
9. To drippings add 1 stick butter.
10. Saute onions and cabbage.
11. Salt and pepper to taste.
12. Blend in hamburger (if using) to pan mixture.

title: Russian Tea Balls
description: Delicate Russian tea balls, made with margarine, powdered sugar, vanilla, flour, and nuts, then baked until light brown and rolled in powdered sugar. These melt-in-your-mouth cookies are perfect for holidays or tea time.
instructions:
1. Cream margarine and sugar.
2. Add other ingredients. Refrigerate for 1 hour.
3. Form into small balls.
4. Place on ungreased sheet.
5. Bake at 400° until light brown.
6. Cool 2 minutes. Roll in powdered sugar while hot and again when cool.

title: Mushroom Caviar
description: Savory mushroom caviar, prepared by cooking onions and mushrooms with coriander, salt, and pepper until most of the liquid evaporates. This flavorful spread is perfect served chilled with black bread.
instructions:
1. Cook onions until soft.
2. Add mushrooms, coriander, salt and pepper.
3. Cook until mushrooms let off their juice.
4. Turn up heat and cook until most of liquid evaporates.
5. Refrigerate until chilled.
6. Add seasoning, if necessary.
7. Garnish with chopped parsley and serve with thinly sliced black bread.
8. Freezes well.

title: Layered Tostada Dip
description: A flavorful layered tostada dip, featuring a base of seasoned beans, topped with a creamy avocado mixture, olives, tomatoes, green chilies, and Monterey Jack cheese. Perfect for sharing at any gathering.
instructions:
1. Combine beans and chili powder.
2. Mix well.
3. Combine avocados, salad dressing, bacon, onion and seasonings.
4. Mix well.
5. Layer bean mixture, avocado mixture, olives, tomato, chilies and cheese in shallow bowl.
6. Serve with tortilla chips.
7. Makes 6 cups.

title: Easy Garden Caesar Dip
description: This easy garden Caesar dip is a quick and creamy blend of cilantro and pepper dressing, perfect for dipping vegetables or chips. Simply combine ingredients in a food processor until smooth.
instructions:
1. Combine ingredients in a food processor or blender and process until smooth. Serve the dip in a shallow bowl or small hollowed out bell pepper.
2. Makes 1 1/4 cups.

title: Latkes(Potato Pancakes)
description: Golden and crispy, these potato pancakes (latkes) are made from grated potatoes, onion, and apple, bound together with egg and flour. Serve them hot with your favorite toppings.
instructions:
1. Peel and grate potatoes, onion and apple on coarse grater. Beat eggs lightly; add grated potatoes, onion and apple and blend well.
2. Add salt, flour and baking powder and mix thoroughly.
3. Pour 1-inch oil into skillet and heat.
4. Drop mix by tablespoons into hot oil.
5. Fry and brown both sides.
6. Serve hot.
7. Drain off any liquid before frying each cake.
8. Serve with sour cream or applesauce.
9. Makes 20.

title: Beet Soup Or Borsch
description: A classic beet soup (Borscht) featuring julienned beets simmered in stock, thickened with a flour and sour cream mixture. This comforting soup is vibrant and flavorful, perfect for a hearty meal.
instructions:
1. Add the sliced beets with juice to the stock with a tablespoon or two of vinegar.
2. In a separate bowl, mix the flour and water to make a paste and add to the sour cream, stirring or mixing with an eggbeater.
3. The trick to not getting lumpy sour cream in soup is to add some hot soup (not boiling) to the flour mixture, a ladle at a time, stirring or mixing as you do this, for 6 or 7 ladles. Add this mixture to the pot of soup, stirring while adding.
4. Make sure the soup is not boiling.
5. If soup is still too sweet, add another tablespoon or two of vinegar or dill pickle juice.

title: Ruben Casserole
description: This savory Ruben casserole layers sauerkraut, corned beef, Swiss cheese, and rye bread, seasoned with caraway seeds and onion. It's a comforting and hearty dish, baked until golden and bubbly.
instructions:
1. Combine kraut, onion, parsley and seed in 13 x 9 x 2-inch pan. Top with 2 cups cheese and 2/3 cup dressing.
2. Layer beef slices. Add rest of cheese and dressing.
3. Toss bread cubes with margarine and spread on top.
4. Bake in 375° oven for 35 minutes.

title: Tortilla Soup(Servings:  8)
description: A flavorful tortilla soup with tender chicken, sautéed vegetables, and a hint of spice from cumin, chili powder, and Tabasco. It's simmered in a rich chicken broth and served over crispy tortilla chips.
instructions:
1. Saute onion, garlic, pepper, celery and spices.
2. Add flour and mix.
3. Gradually add tomatoes, carrots, chicken, broth and add water as needed.
4. Cook until done.
5. Serve over tortilla chips. May add grated cheese or sour cream, but this adds fat.

title: Russian Chicken
description: Effortless Russian chicken baked with a sweet and savory sauce made from apricot preserves, onion soup mix, and dressing. This simple recipe yields tender, flavorful chicken perfect for a weeknight dinner.
instructions:
1. Arrange chicken pieces in baking dish (skin side down).
2. Heat apricot preserves, soup mix and dressing in saucepan.
3. After preserves are melted, pour over chicken.
4. Cover with foil and bake at 350° for 1/2 hour.
5. Remove foil and turn chicken over. Continue baking for another 1/2 hour.
6. Good served with white rice.

title: E Z Chicken Strips
description: Quick and easy chicken strips, coated in a seasoned flour and egg batter, then cooked until golden. These tender and juicy strips are perfect for a snack or light meal.
instructions:
1. Cut chicken breasts into strips.
2. Mix flour, salt, pepper and baking powder together.
3. In a medium size bowl, combine milk and egg.
4. Beat well.
5. Combine the flour mixture to egg mixture, coating on all sides of chicken.
6. Place in large skillet with oil or shortening and deep fat until strips are all golden in color.

title: Kolbasi And Sauerkraut
description: A traditional dish combining savory kolbasi sausage with tangy sauerkraut, simmered with onions and salt pork. This comforting meal is hearty and full of robust flavors.
instructions:
1. Bake kolbasi in 325° oven for 1 hour in pan with water to cover.
2. Thoroughly rinse and drain sauerkraut.
3. Place in part with 2 whole onions and salt pork; add salt and pepper to taste.
4. Add water to cover; simmer 45 minutes.
5. When kolbasi is cooked, drain and cut into 4-inch pieces.
6. Add to sauerkraut and continue cooking for 30 minutes.
7. Brown flour in bacon fat.
8. When golden brown, add to kolbasi and sauerkraut and mix thoroughly.

title: Creole Chicken And Mushroom Salad
description: A vibrant Creole chicken and mushroom salad featuring tender chicken, fresh vegetables, and a zesty lemon-red wine vinaigrette. This refreshing salad is perfect for a light lunch or dinner.
instructions:
1. Line a bowl with lettuce.
2. Toss the first 6 ingredients together and place in the lettuce-lined bowl.
3. Mix the last 6 ingredients well.
4. Pour over the salad and toss lightly.

title: Ommy'S Russian Salad Dressing
description: This easy Russian salad dressing combines sweet and tangy flavors from catsup, sugar, vinegar, and a hint of garlic and onion. It's perfect for enhancing your favorite greens.
instructions:
1. Mix all together, except the oil.
2. Add oil slowly and beat thoroughly.
3. Makes 1 cup of dressing.

title: Russian Mints
description: Decadent Russian mints made with a creamy butter-sugar base, flavored with peppermint and chocolate, layered with vanilla wafers. These delightful treats are topped with whipped cream and cherries.
instructions:
1. Cream butter and powdered sugar together.
2. Add eggs, one at a time, beating after each.
3. Add flavoring and chocolate.
4. Spoon half of wafers into bottom of cupcake liners.
5. Spoon the chocolate mixture on top and cover with the rest of wafer crumbs.
6. Top with dab of whipped cream and cherry.
7. Place in freezer.
8. Makes about 20.

title: Sweet-Sour Kraut And Pork Chops
description: This sweet-sour kraut and pork chops dish features tender pork and potatoes baked with tangy sauerkraut and sweet pineapple. A comforting and flavorful meal, perfect for a cozy evening.
instructions: 1. In 12 x 7 x 2-inch baking dish, combine potato slices and onion. Add water. Cover and bake in 350° oven for 45 minutes or until nearly tender.

title: Melissa'S Tortellini With Vegetables
description: Fresh tortellini tossed with sautéed mushrooms, tomatoes, garlic, and onion, seasoned with soy sauce and fresh basil. A quick and delicious vegetarian dish that's full of flavor.
instructions:
1. Cook tortellini according to package directions.
2. Meanwhile, heat olive oil in a medium size skillet.
3. Stir in spices.
4. Add garlic.
5. Stir in onion and mushrooms.
6. Cook and stir until onion is tender.
7. Add sun-dried tomatoes, which have been placed in boiling water for 2 minutes to soften.
8. Drain tortellini and serve with stir-fried vegetables.
9. Add cheese or soy sauce to your taste (optional).

title: No Peek Brisket
description: A flavorful brisket slow-cooked on a bed of rye bread with chile sauce, beer, onions, and garlic. This tender and juicy brisket is perfect for a hearty family meal.
instructions:
1. Lay slices of rye bread in bottom of Dutch oven.
2. Place brisket, with fat side down, on top of the bread in Dutch oven. Sprinkle garlic powder and pepper on brisket.
3. Throw onion slices into pan.
4. Pour beer over the meat.
5. Spread the chile sauce over the meat.
6. Cover Dutch oven.

title: Chicken - Polish Style(Kurczeta Po Polsku)
description: Chicken Polish style (Kurczeta Po Polsku) features a whole chicken seasoned and stuffed with a savory bread crumb and chicken liver mixture. It's a traditional and flavorful roasted chicken dish.
instructions:
1. Sprinkle the chicken with salt; let stand 1 hour.
2. Chop the livers
3. finely.
4. Combine
5. with bread crumbs, egg, salt to taste, dill
6. weed,
7. pepper
8. and
9. as much milk as needed for a loose, sour cream-like consistency.
10. Fill cavity
11. of
12. chicken with
13. crumb mixture; truss.
14. Place chicken
15. in	roasting
16. pan. Bake at 400° for about 45 minutes, or until chicken is tender. Baste often with melted butter.

title: Broccoli And Mushroom Salad
description: A crisp broccoli and mushroom salad with celery and onion, dressed in a tangy vinaigrette with paprika and sugar. This refreshing salad is simple to prepare and full of texture.
instructions:
1. Mix vegetables together and let set for 1 hour.
2. Mix sugar, oil and etc. together and pour over vegetables.
3. Let stand 1 hour before serving.

title: Peasant Rye
description: A creamy peasant rye bread dip filled with a mixture of mayonnaise, sour cream, dill, garlic, and olives. Perfect for scooping with torn rye bread, it's a delightful appetizer.
instructions:
1. Mix all ingredients, except the bread.
2. Chill mixture for 2 to 3 hours.
3. Then cut top of bread off and pull bread out of inside of loaf to form a cavity.
4. Fill cavity of bread with dip mixture before serving.
5. Use bread cubes for dipping.

title: Potato Pancake Appetizers
description: Crispy potato pancake appetizers featuring grated potatoes, carrots, and zucchini, served with a dollop of low-fat sour cream and fresh herbs. These savory bites are perfect for any gathering.
instructions: 1. Preheat oven to 425°. Wrap potatoes in several layers of paper towels; squeeze to remove excess moisture. Beat egg, flour, salt and pepper in large bowl. Add potatoes, carrot and zucchini; mix well. Oil 2 nonstick baking sheets. Place vegetable mixture by heaping spoonfuls onto baking sheets; flatten slightly. Bake 8 to 15 minutes, until bottoms are browned. Turn; bake 5 to 10 minutes more. Stir together sour cream and herbs; serve with warm potatoes.

title: Reuben Meatballs
description: Flavorful Reuben meatballs made with ground beef, sauerkraut, caraway seeds, and rice, baked until tender. These savory meatballs are a creative twist on a classic sandwich, perfect for a hearty meal.
instructions:
1. In a bowl, combine the egg, onion, bread crumbs, parsley, salt and pepper. Stir in rice. Crumble beef over the mixture and mix well. Shape into 15 balls. Place in an ungreased 13 x 9 x 2 inch baking dish. Bake uncovered at 350° for 15-20 minutes or until browned; drain. Arrange sauerkraut over meatballs; sprinkle with caraway seeds. Combine soup and salad dressing; spread over the top. Cover and bake for 35-45 minutes or until meat is no longer pink. Uncover; sprinkle
2. with Swiss cheese, place back in oven until cheese is melted. Serve with rye bread, if desired.

title: Lazy Man'S Stuffed Cabbage
description: Hearty "Lazy Man's" cabbage rolls are deconstructed and baked in a casserole, featuring a savory filling of ground beef and rice simmered in a rich tomato sauce with tender cabbage.
instructions:
1. Put cabbage in boiling water; cook until slightly soft. Drain.
2. Brown ground beef and onions until onions are clear and beef loses its pink.
3. Drain.
4. Add tomato soup, tomato sauce and water to meat.
5. Prepare Minute rice as directed on package.
6. Add meat and tomato mixture.
7. In an 11 x 15-inch baking pan, layer cabbage and meat-tomato mixture; repeat once more.
8. Cover with aluminum foil; bake in a 350° oven for 1 hour.

title: Blintzes Or Crepes (Pancakes)
description: Delicate, thin blintzes (or crepes) are pan-fried until golden, perfect for wrapping a sweet, creamy filling of eggs, sugar, and cinnamon. A delightful treat for any time of day.
instructions:
1. Mix together all ingredients until smooth.
2. Grease an 8-inch skillet lightly with 1 teaspoon vegetable shortening, heat, pour enough batter to coat bottom.
3. Tilt skillet to cover evenly and cook until delicate brown.
4. Loosen and remove with spatula. Continue until all batter is used up.

title: Thin White Sauce
description: A classic thin white sauce, made with a simple roux of butter and flour, is slowly thickened with milk and seasoned with salt. Ideal as a base for many dishes.
instructions:
1. Combine butter and flour.
2. Add milk slowly, stirring constantly.
3. Cook over hot water until thick and smooth.
4. Add salt and pepper.

title: Pork Chops L'Orange
description: Tender pork chops are seasoned, browned, and then baked over a bed of rice infused with orange juice and chicken stock. This creates a flavorful, aromatic main course.
instructions:
1. Season and brown chops on the top of the stove.
2. Spray a 9 x 13-inch pan with cooking spray.
3. Sprinkle rice in pan.
4. Pour orange juice over rice.
5. Add chops on top.
6. Pour chicken stock (well salted) over chops.
7. Cover with foil and bake for 1 hour in a 350° oven.

title: Borsch
description: A rich and vibrant borscht, featuring beef stock, hearty vegetables like beets and cabbage, and a savory sauteed onion base with honey and vinegar for a nuanced flavor. A true taste of tradition.
instructions:
1. Place stock, vegetables and seasonings in soup kettle.
2. Bring to a boil; reduce heat, then cover and simmer 45 minutes. Saute chopped onion in oil until golden; remove from heat and add honey, vinegar, tomato and Tamari. Stir mixture into soup. Garnish soup with sour cream or plain yogurt; serve with dark rye bread. Yields 2 1/2 to 3 quarts. Best made a few hours in advance.

title: Russian Corn Bread
description: This unique "Russian Corn Bread" combines cornmeal, cream-style corn, potatoes, and cheese, baked into a flavorful and moist bread with a hint of spice from hot peppers.
instructions: 1. Mix and bake at 375° for 45 minutes.

title: Texas Torte(Cheese Appetizers)
description: Cheesy and savory Texas Torte appetizers are made with eggs, flour, milk, green chilies, and two types of cheddar cheese, baked until golden and bubbly. An easy crowd-pleaser.
instructions:
1. Preheat oven to 350°.
2. To eggs, add flour, salt and milk. Beat well.
3. Add remaining ingredients and mix well.
4. Pour into a flat well-greased 8 x 12-inch Pyrex baking dish.
5. Bake at 350° for 35 minutes.
6. Cut into squares and serve hot.
7. Freezes well.
8. It doesn't double well.

title: Bavarian Kielbasa With Noodles
description: A comforting dish of Bavarian kielbasa and onion, browned and then simmered in a creamy mushroom soup sauce, served over tender egg noodles. Quick and satisfying.
ingredients: Campbell's condensed cream of mushroom soup
instructions:
1. Cook noodles according to package directions; drain. Meanwhile, in a 10-inch skillet over medium heat, cook kielbasa and onion until browned, stirring often.
2. Spoon off fat.
3. Add soup; stir until smooth.
4. Gradually stir in milk.
5. Add beans and pepper.
6. Heat to boiling.
7. Reduce heat to low.
8. Cover; cook 5 minutes or until beans are tender, stirring occasionally.
9. Stir cooked noodles into skillet.
10. Heat 2 minutes, stirring often. Serve with mustard, if desired (spicy brown mustard or French mustard).
11. Makes 4 servings.

title: Pizza Rounds
description: Savory pizza rounds feature a mixture of browned ground beef, sausage, melted Velveeta cheese, and Italian seasonings spread on party rye bread. A fantastic easy appetizer.
instructions:
1. Brown and drain meats.
2. Combine meats with cheese until cheese is melted.
3. Add all remaining ingredients, except bread, and mix until thoroughly mixed.
4. Spread mixture on each slice of bread. Place on cookie sheet and freeze.
5. Transfer, when frozen, to freezer bags.
6. Use as many as needed.
7. When you bake them, bake on a cookie sheet at 350° for 15 minutes.
8. May be kept frozen indefinitely.

title: Marion'S Rye Bread
description: A homemade rye bread recipe featuring rye flour, molasses, and a unique method involving boiling water to activate yeast. This yields a dense and flavorful loaf with a rustic appeal.
instructions:
1. Pour 1 quart boiling water on top.
2. Mix well.
3. When cooled to lukewarm (115°), add 1 package dry yeast, dissolved in 1/4 cup warm water.
4. Add white flour (takes quite a bit).
5. Let rise, make into 4 loaves, prick with a fork and let rise again.
6. Bake at 350° for 35 minutes.

title: South Of The Border
description: "South of the Border" is an easy casserole layering crunchy corn chips, onions, kidney beans, olives, and a savory meat alternative with tomato and taco seasoning. A flavorful, satisfying meal.
instructions:
1. Line bottom of a 5-quart casserole with half the corn chips. Layer the remaining ingredients in the following order: onion, kidney beans, olives, burger and remaining chips.
2. In a small mixing bowl, combine tomato sauce and taco seasoning mix.
3. Pour the sauce over the top of the layered mixture.
4. Put cheese on top. Bake at 350° for 35 to 45 minutes.
5. Serves 8.

title: Quick Chocolate Buttermilk Cake
description: A quick and easy chocolate buttermilk cake that's rich, moist, and deliciously comforting. This recipe is perfect for satisfying chocolate cravings with minimal effort.
instructions:
1. Sift first 3 ingredients together.
2. In a saucepan, bring butter/margarine, water, shortening, buttermilk and cocoa to a boil.
3. Remove from heat.
4. When mixture is cool, pour over sifted dry ingredients and mix well.
5. Add beaten eggs and soda.
6. Bake at 400° for 20 to 25 minutes for layer cakes; 28 to 32 minutes for 9 x 13-inch pan.

title: Crab Cheese Puff
description: Delectable crab cheese puffs start with a creamy crab and cheese mixture, spread over toasted muffin halves, and then broiled until warm and golden. An irresistible appetizer.
instructions:
1. Mix ingredients together, except muffins.
2. Cut eight muffins in half; toast under broiler.
3. Spread thin layer of crab meat mixture on each muffin piece.
4. Put under broiler until spread starts to bubble.
5. Serve hot.
6. This is a great appetizer.

title: World'S Easiest Chicken
description: The "World's Easiest Chicken" features chicken breasts baked under a rich, creamy sauce made from a blend of condensed soups, Parmesan cheese, and almonds. A simple, comforting dinner.
instructions:
1. Preheat oven to 350°.
2. Place chicken in a 9 x 13-inch casserole.
3. Mix remaining ingredients and pour over chicken. Bake, uncovered, for about 1 hour and 15 minutes.
4. Serve with rice.
5. It's great.
6. Serves 4 to 6.
7. Lite soups can be used.

title: Tomato And Mushroom Salad
description: A refreshing salad combining juicy cherry tomatoes, earthy fresh mushrooms, and crisp green onions, all tossed in a tangy vinaigrette dressing. Perfect for a light meal or side dish.
instructions:
1. Combine tomatoes, mushrooms and onions.
2. Mix the rest of the ingredients together.
3. Pour over and stir.
4. Chill for a couple of hours before serving.

title: Borscht(Beet Soup)
description: This quick beet soup, or borscht, combines sautéed onions with beef broth, beet juice, and cabbage for a vibrant and warming meal. Best served with a dollop of sour cream and crispy saltines.
instructions:
1. In a pan, saute onions in butter.
2. Add to pan with all the other ingredients.
3. Cover and cook about 10 minutes or until cabbage is tender.
4. Serve with sour cream saltines.
5. Can be eaten without cream.
6. You can add more cabbage and beets to recipe.

title: Stroganoff Bread
description: A comforting take on Stroganoff, served hot on toasted French bread, topped with melted cheese, green onions, and fresh tomatoes. Quick and satisfying, perfect for a cozy meal.
instructions:
1. Cut French bread in half.
2. Toast at 375° for 5 to 10 minutes. Brown meat with green onion.
3. Stir in sour cream.
4. Mix in milk, Worcestershire sauce and garlic salt.
5. Heat mixture, but do not boil.

title: Trail Cookies
description: These delightful trail cookies are packed with wholesome oats, rich peanut butter, and sweet chocolate chips and raisins. A perfect snack for on-the-go or a sweet treat at home.
instructions:
1. Stir flour, soda and salt; set aside.
2. In large bowl, beat butter, peanut butter and sugars until creamy.
3. Beat in eggs, milk and vanilla.
4. Stir in flour mixture, then oats, chocolate and raisins.

title: Tangy Minute Steak
description: Effortlessly delicious, this tangy minute steak recipe features a simple marinade of onion soup, mushroom soup, and a splash of soda. Baked until tender, it's a quick fix for a tasty dinner.
instructions:
1. Mix all ingredients together.
2. Pour over minute steak and cook in oven at 350° for approximately 1 hour.

title: Bran 'N Beef Stroganoff
description: A unique twist on traditional Stroganoff, featuring ground beef patties enhanced with Bran Flakes for extra texture and flavor. Simmered in a creamy sauce and served over buttered noodles, it's a comforting and hearty meal.
instructions:
1. In large bowl beat egg until foamy.
2. Stir in milk, Bran Flakes, salt, pepper and paprika.
3. Let stand 1 to 2 minutes.
4. Add ground beef, mixing until combined.
5. Form into 20 (1 1/2-inch) meatballs. Bake in 400° oven about 15 minutes; drain.

title: Russian Cream
description: Experience the classic elegance of Russian Cream, a delicate, chilled dessert. This smooth and creamy gelatin-based treat is subtly sweet with a hint of vanilla and a tangy touch of sour cream.
instructions:
1. Heat cream with sugar; soak gelatin in cold water; combine with hot cream.
2. Cool until slightly thickened.
3. Whip sour cream and vanilla and combine as first mixture congeals.
4. Pour into ring mold.
5. Chill until firm.

title: Roast Pork With Sauerkraut
description: A savory pork roast slow-cooked with flavorful sauerkraut, tart apples, and sweet brown sugar for a delightful balance of tastes. This hearty dish is perfect for a comforting meal.
instructions:
1. Preheat oven to 450°.
2. Rub roast with salt and pepper.
3. Put in large roasting pan; put in oven, turning to brown, for 20 to 30 minutes.
4. Remove roast; lower oven to 250°.
5. Scatter sauerkraut around roast.
6. In a bowl, mix the apples, onion, sugar, mustard and caraway seed.
7. Sprinkle this mixture over sauerkraut and drizzle wine over top.
8. (Can be refrigerated at this point and roasted later or next day.)
9. Cover and roast at 250° for about 4 hours, or until tender.
10. Moisten with more wine if it becomes dry.
11. Let rest 10 minutes before slicing.
12. Serves 4 to 6 people.

title: Sauerkraut Bake
description: A simple yet savory sauerkraut bake featuring crispy bacon, sweet onions, and ripe tomatoes, all harmoniously combined. This quick side dish offers a delightful balance of textures and flavors.
instructions: 1. In a large skillet, fry bacon over medium heat until crisp. Drain on paper towel. Crumble and set aside.

title: Russian Salad
description: A delightful and unusual 'Russian Salad' that's more dessert than savory, combining marshmallows, lime gelatin, cream cheese, and fruit. Chill until set for a unique sweet treat.
instructions:
1. Mix marshmallows, Jell-O and cream cheese with hot water; dissolve over low heat.
2. Remove from heat.
3. Add other ingredients. Chill in molds until set.

title: Stuffed Green Peppers(And Stuffed Zucchini And Stuffed Cabbage Rolls)
description: Hearty stuffed peppers (or zucchini/cabbage rolls) filled with a savory mixture of sausage, rice, and herbs, simmered in a tomato-based sauce. A comforting and versatile dish for any occasion.
instructions:
1. Halve the peppers lengthwise, removing stem ends, seeds and membranes.
2. Immerse peppers in boiling water for 3 minutes. Sprinkle insides with salt and invert on paper towels to drain. In a skillet, cook the sausage or ground beef with the onion until the meat is brown and the onion is tender.
3. Drain the fat.
4. Stir in the undrained tomatoes, uncooked rice, worcestershire sauce, water, and salt and pepper.
5. Bring to a boil then reduce the heat, cover, and simmer for 15-18 minutes, or until the rice is tender. Stir in 1/4 c. cheese.
6. Fill the peppers with the meat mixture and place them in an 8 x 8 inch baking dish with any remaining meat mixture.
7. Bake at 375° about 15 minutes, or until heated through. Sprinkle with the remaining cheese.
8. Let stand a couple minutes before serving.

title: Cold Beet Soup-Russia(Svekolnik)
description: Svekolnik, a traditional Russian cold beet soup, is a refreshingly tangy and earthy dish. Made with chicken stock, beets, potatoes, and lemon, it's perfect for a light and flavorful meal.
instructions: 1. Bring chicken stock and water to a boil with the vegetables, garlic salt, and pepper. Simmer 10-15 minutes. Stir in the sugar. Continue cooking for 30 more minutes. Let soup cool to room temperature, then stir in lemon juice. Chill soup in refrigerator for several hours or overnight. Garnish with chopped parsley and sour cream.

title: Grandma "Field'S" Cabbage Rolls Or Stuffed Cabbage
description: Classic cabbage rolls, or Golabki, filled with a hearty mixture of ground beef and pork, rice, and seasonings, simmered in a rich tomato sauce. A beloved comfort food, perfect for family dinners.
instructions: 1. Please see note below before adding all other ingredients.

title: Dijon Chicken
description: Quick and flavorful Dijon chicken breasts, coated in a zesty sour cream and mustard mixture, then breaded and baked to golden perfection. A simple yet elegant main course.
instructions:
1. Lightly sprinkle chicken with salt, pepper and garlic powder. Combine sour cream and mustard in a shallow dish, mixing well.
2. Dip each chicken breast into mustard mixture and dredge in breadcrumbs. Arrange chicken in single layer in baking pans.
3. Bake at 375° for 50 minutes.

title: Betty Ann'S Cabbage Rolls
description: Betty Ann's comforting cabbage rolls feature a savory filling of hamburger, rice, and egg, wrapped in tender cabbage leaves. Simmered in a sweet and tangy tomato sauce with sauerkraut, they're a true family favorite.
instructions:
1. Mix hamburger, egg and rice.
2. Salt and pepper to taste.
3. Add 2 or 3 tablespoons tomato sauce.
4. Put a spoonful in each cabbage leaf.
5. Roll leaf around meat tightly and secure with wooden toothpicks.
6. Put cabbage rolls on rack in pressure cooker.
7. Pour rest of tomato sauce over rolls.
8. Mix brown sugar, vinegar and water; pour over rolls.
9. Cook 10 minutes in pressure cooker or 1 1/2 hours without pressure.
10. After done, add sauerkraut, if desired.
11. Simmer 10 minutes.

title: Pressure Cooker Spareribs And Sauerkraut
description: Succulent spareribs cooked to tender perfection in a pressure cooker with tangy sauerkraut and sweet onions. A hearty and flavorful meal that's surprisingly quick to prepare.
instructions:
1. Brown spareribs in frying pan with oil or under broiler. Place trivet in pressure cooker. Pour in water and add sauerkraut, onions and spareribs. Cook at high pressure for 1 hour.
2. Serve with your choice of potato, etc.

title: Waffles "Sinless"
description: Light and fluffy 'Sinless' waffles, a guilt-free indulgence for breakfast or brunch. Made with simple ingredients, they are perfect for a quick and satisfying start to your day.
instructions:
1. Combine all ingredients.
2. Pour into hot waffle iron.
3. (Spray with butter flavor Pam first.)

title: Crustless Quiche
description: A classic crustless quiche, featuring a savory blend of eggs, milk, and Cheddar cheese, studded with sautéed mushrooms and onions. Perfect for brunch or a light meal.
instructions:
1. Saute mushrooms and onions in shortening.
2. Mix all in bowl with whisk; reserve 2 ounces of the cheese.
3. Pour into greased 9 or 10-inch pie plate; bake in 425° oven for 25 minutes.
4. Remove from oven and spread remaining cheese on top; bake 5 minutes more.

title: Polish Stuffed Cabbage
description: Traditional Polish Stuffed Cabbage (Gołąbki) with tender cabbage leaves wrapped around a savory filling of rice, onion, and spices. Simmered in a sweet tomato soup, these rolls are a comforting and hearty meal.
instructions:
1. Core head of cabbage.
2. Set cabbage hole down in large pot with 1 or 2 inches of water and steam.
3. Pull off leaves, one by one (total of about 22 to 26).
4. Cook rice.
5. Saute chopped onion in butter.
6. Mix meat loaf mixture with the rice and onion mixture with your hands.
7. Add eggs to the mixture and salt and pepper to taste.
8. Take each cabbage leaf and put some meat in the middle. Wrap envelope-style tightly.
9. Put 1 small box of raisins on bottom of pan.
10. Tightly pack 2 rows of cabbage balls in same pot as you boiled cabbage.
11. Sprinkle another few raisins on top.
12. Add tomato soup and 2 cans water.
13. Cover and bring to a boil, then reduce to a simmer for 1 1/2 hours.
14. Serve with mashed potatoes and cucumber salad.

title: Cabbage-Sausage-Beef Goulash
description: A hearty Cabbage-Sausage-Beef Goulash, combining browned meats, tender cabbage, and a zesty tomato sauce with a hint of chili powder. A warming and satisfying one-pot meal.
instructions:
1. In a large kettle, brown sausage, beef and onion.
2. Drain well. Add remaining ingredients except cabbage, and mix well.
3. Stir in cabbage and simmer 20 to 25 minutes or until cabbage is tender. Serve with French bread and a pot of pinto beans

title: Chipped Beef Gravy(Fat-Free)
description: A quick and easy chipped beef gravy, made fat-free by rinsing the beef. It's a simple, savory sauce perfect for a busy morning.
instructions:
1. Tear beef into small pieces.
2. Place in colander and rinse to remove some of the salt.

title: Cherry Icebox Pie
description: A delightful no-bake cherry icebox pie featuring layers of graham crackers, sweet cherries, and a creamy filling. Ideal for a refreshing dessert without turning on the oven.
instructions:
1. Line 8 x 8 dish with whole graham crackers.
2. Drain juice from cherries and set juice aside.
3. Spread drained cherries over whole graham crackers in dish.
4. Sprinkle sugar over cherries and set aside.

title: Duck And Sauerkraut
description: Braised duck breasts served over tangy sauerkraut, baked to tender perfection. This dish offers a rich, savory flavor contrast that is simple to prepare.
instructions:
1. Parboil breasts.
2. Place sauerkraut in casserole.
3. Arrange breasts over kraut.
4. Cover.
5. Bake at 325° for 1 hour or until tender.

title: Alavna'S E Z Burritos
description: Easy beef burritos filled with a flavorful mixture of roast beef, gravy, cheese, and fresh vegetables. A convenient and satisfying meal for any day of the week.
instructions:
1. Over low flame, in 5-quart cooking pot, add cans of roast beef and gravy, chopped green bell pepper and 2 diced tomatoes (save the last tomato for adding to Burritos when rolled), 1 teaspoon ground cumin, 1 teaspoon garlic powder, 1 teaspoon seasoning salt and 1 teaspoon pepper.
2. Cook over low flame, covered, until bell pepper is soft.
3. Stir occasionally.
4. When meat mixture is cooked, heat tortillas, then one tortilla at a time.
5. Add meat mixture, 2 tablespoons cheese, 1 tablespoon tomatoes, 1 tablespoon shredded lettuce and roll.
6. You can also add your own favorite taco sauce.

title: Sausage-Cheese Hors D'Oeuvres
description: Savory sausage and Velveeta cheese mixture, seasoned with Worcestershire and Tabasco, spread on party rye bread. These bite-sized hors d'oeuvres are quick to make and perfect for entertaining.
instructions:
1. Remove sausage skin and fry meat.
2. Drain off fat.
3. Add Worcestershire and Tabasco sauces.
4. Cut Velveeta cheese into small cubes and melt in with sausage mixture.
5. Spread on slices of party rye bread and place on a cookie sheet.
6. Bake at 350° for 10 to 15 minutes until browned.
7. These can be made the day before and stored in the refrigerator until ready to be baked.

title: Party Feta Appetizer
description: A simple and delicious feta cheese appetizer spread on toasted party rye bread. This quick broiled snack is ideal for last-minute gatherings.
instructions:
1. Combine all ingredients, except bread slices.
2. Toast one side of bread slices.
3. Spread mayonnaise mixture onto untoasted side of bread slices.
4. Broil 1 1/2 to 2 minutes or until lightly browned and bubbly.
5. Serve.
6. Makes 12 appetizers.

title: Scrumptious Ice Cream Torte
description: An impressive ice cream torte layered with ladyfingers, chocolate and vanilla ice cream, and Heath bar crunch. A perfect chilled dessert for any occasion, requiring some freezing time.
instructions:
1. Crush Heath bars. Soften ice cream. Split ladyfingers in half and line the bottom and sides of a 10 or 12-inch spring-form pan. Spoon layer of chocolate ice cream over ladyfingers. Cover with half of crushed Heath bars. Add layer of vanilla ice cream.
2. Cover with remaining Heath bars. Add top layer of peppermint ice cream. Freeze at least 8 hours.
3. Unmold and top with sweetened whipped cream. Serves 8 to 10.

title: Mushroom Salad
description: A fresh mushroom salad tossed with Cheddar cheese, green onions, and a simple oil and vinegar dressing. This quick and refreshing side dish is perfect for any meal.
instructions:
1. Mix mushrooms, cheese and green onion together.
2. Then mix the oil, vinegar and Cavender's Greek seasoning for dressing.
3. After mixing well, pour over mushroom mixture.
4. Serves 10.

title: Mushroom Salad(France)
description: A classic French-inspired mushroom salad, featuring sliced mushrooms dressed with lemon, garlic, and a light mayonnaise-based dressing. This elegant side is quick and refreshing.
instructions:
1. Clean mushrooms; wash in cold water to which vinegar has been added.
2. Dry in a cloth; slice.
3. To keep mushrooms from turning black, sprinkle with lemon juice.
4. Toss lightly but thoroughly with mixture of mayonnaise, garlic, salt and pepper. Place in refrigerator for 1 or 2 hours; mixture will become moist from juice of mushrooms.
5. Stir lightly several times during chilling. Serve on a bed of bibb lettuce and parsley.
6. Yields 10 to 12 servings.

title: Susan'S Favorite Enchiladas
description: Flavorful chicken enchiladas, simply garnished with extra cheese, sour cream, and fresh cilantro. A comforting and customizable dish for any weeknight.
instructions:
1. Garnishes:
2. Additional cheese and sour cream and chopped cilantro.

title: Sauteed Butternut Squash
description: Tender sautéed butternut squash seasoned with garlic, fresh sage, and parsley. This simple side dish is perfect for a healthy and flavorful addition to any meal.
instructions:
1. In a heavy (preferably nonstick) skillet, heat oil until sizzling.
2. Add squash and garlic to coat well with oil.
3. Saute slowly over low heat, stirring frequently, for about 30 minutes until squash is golden and tender.
4. (Add a tablespoon or two of water to pan if squash begins to stick.)
5. Add salt and pepper to taste, then sprinkle sage and parsley over squash and mix to combine well.
6. Serves 4.

title: Zesty Sauerkraut
description: Zesty sauerkraut enhanced with sautéed onions, crumbled bacon, beer, molasses, and mustard. This flavorful side dish offers a unique twist on traditional sauerkraut.
instructions:
1. Saute onion and pepper in butter.
2. Broil bacon and crumble. Rinse sauerkraut and place in baking dish.
3. Add the beer, molasses and mustard to onions, peppers and bacon.
4. Cook and stir for 3 minutes.
5. Pour over sauerkraut and bake 30 minutes at 350°.

title: Cherry Torte
description: A delightful cherry torte featuring a tender crust, sweet cherry pie filling, and a meringue-like topping with ground nuts. Perfect for a comforting dessert.
instructions:
1. Spread out on cookie sheet and make edges up side.
2. Cover with cherry pie filling.
3. Beat egg whites until stiff.
4. Add 6 tablespoons sugar.
5. Cover filling and sprinkle with ground nuts. Bake at 350° for 25 to 30 minutes.

title: Polish Mistakes
description: Quick and savory mini open-faced sandwiches with a hot sausage and hamburger mixture, topped with melted Cheez Whiz. Broiled to perfection on party rye bread, these are great for appetizers.
instructions:
1. Fry sausage and hamburger together; drain grease off.
2. Put in a bowl while warm; mix Cheez Whiz.
3. Put on party rye bread (rye bread) and broil until bread is lightly brown and crisp.
4. Watch close.

title: Polish Pickle Soup
description: A hearty Polish pickle soup (Zupa Ogórkowa) made with pork hocks, vegetables like potatoes and cabbage, and a distinctive tang from pickle juice and dill. A comforting and traditional meal.
instructions:
1. Put water in kettle and then add pork hocks and onion.
2. Cook 1 hour.
3. Remove pork hocks and cool.
4. When cooled, dice meat and set aside.
5. Add potato, cabbage and carrots to kettle.
6. Cook until tender.
7. Then add pickle juice, pickles, dill weed, pepper and diced meat.
8. Cook until heated through.
9. Serves 6 to 8.

title: Crisp Squash-And-Pepper Toss
description: A flavorful and colorful toss of yellow squash, red pepper, and kielbasa, seasoned with herbs and red wine vinegar. Quickly cooked, this dish makes a satisfying main or side.
instructions:
1. Combine basil, oregano, thyme, salt, pepper, vinegar and water in a shallow 2 1/2-quart casserole; add squash.
2. Cover with heavy-duty plastic wrap; microwave at High for 3 to 4 minutes. Let stand, covered, 3 to 4 minutes.
3. Add onion, garlic, sweet red pepper and olive oil; toss gently.
4. Cover and microwave at High for 2 to 4 minutes.
5. Add sausage; toss gently.
6. Cover and microwave at High for 1 to 2 minutes.
7. Yields 6 to 8 servings.

title: Reuben Salad
description: A deconstructed Reuben sandwich presented as a refreshing salad with layers of sour kraut, dill pickle, roast beef, and Swiss cheese. All tossed with a savory dressing.
instructions:
1. Mix all together.
2. Layer half of lettuce, half of sour kraut, dill pickle and parsley mix, half of roast beef, half of Swiss cheese and spread with half of the dressing.
3. Repeat layers and serve sprinkled with rye croutons or with toasted slices of rye bread.

title: Chicken-Sausage Cassoulet
description: A slow-cooked chicken and Polish sausage cassoulet, rich with vegetables, beans, and savory seasonings. This hearty one-pot meal is perfect for a comforting dinner.
instructions:
1. In saucepan, combine carrots, onion and water.
2. Simmer, covered, 5 minutes.
3. Transfer to crock-pot.
4. Stir in tomato paste, wine and seasonings.
5. Add beans.
6. Place frozen chicken and sausage atop beans.
7. Cover; cook on low for 9 to 10 hours or on high for 5 1/2 to 6 hours.

title: Walnut And Zucchini Stuffed Chicken
description: Succulent chicken breasts stuffed with a flavorful mixture of walnuts, zucchini, spinach, and Parmesan cheese, baked until golden and delicious. A savory and satisfying main course.
instructions:
1. Mix cheese, bread crumbs, salt, pepper and walnuts; set aside. Mix zucchini, onion and spinach in another bowl.
2. In skillet, melt butter and stir-fry zucchini mixture on medium-high heat until dry.
3. Remove pan from heat and stir in cheese mixture until thick.
4. Add cream and stir on low heat for 1 minute until creamy. Add lemon juice; taste and adjust seasonings.
5. Slide fingers under chicken skin and spread stuffing under skin on each breast.
6. Bake chicken, skin side up, in a lightly oiled baking dish for 15 minutes in 350° oven.
7. Baste skin with pan juices and bake 10 minutes more until skin crisps.

title: Beef Stroganoff Casserole With Biscuit Topping
description: A comforting casserole combining seasoned ground beef, onions, and garlic in a creamy mushroom and sour cream sauce, topped with a fluffy biscuit crust. A hearty family meal.
instructions:
1. Brown meat and onion with seasonings in a large skillet.
2. Add soup and milk; simmer 10 minutes.
3. Add sour cream and vegetables. Pour into 2-quart baking dish.
4. Put casserole dish in 350° oven while preparing biscuits.
5. Remove from oven.
6. Arrange biscuits on top of mixture.
7. Bake until biscuits are done.
8. Makes 6 servings.

title: Braided Bread (Challah)(Makes Two Loaves)
description: Rich and slightly sweet braided challah bread, perfect for special occasions or a delicious everyday treat. This recipe yields two beautiful loaves with a golden, poppy-seed sprinkled crust.
instructions:
1. In large bowl, dissolve yeast and sugar in water.
2. Stir in one cup flour, salt, two eggs and oil.
3. Add remaining flour gradually, stirring until combined.
4. Turn out onto a lightly floured board. Knead 8 to 10 minutes until smooth and elastic (adding small amounts of flour as needed).
5. (Pretend you are making pottery.) Put dough in a large oil-coated bowl, turning over once to coat whole ball.
6. Cover bowl with large dish cloth and let rise in warm place for one hour.
7. Punch down dough, turn over in bowl, re-cover with cloth and let rise another 45 minutes.
8. Punch down again, place dough on lightly floured board and cut in two.

title: Flavored Mayo
description: A quick and versatile flavored mayonnaise, perfect for sandwiches, dips, or as a creamy addition to casseroles. Easily blended in minutes for an instant flavor boost.
instructions:
1. Put together in a blender and blend.
2. Keep in tight jar in the refrigerator for use on sandwiches, in casserole, for dips, etc.

title: Easy Meatloaf
description: An easy-to-prepare meatloaf using lean hamburger, seasoned and baked until tender. A classic comfort food that's simple to assemble for a weeknight dinner.
instructions: 1. Combine all ingredients thoroughly in large mixing bowl. Place in either a loaf pan or shape into loaf in large baking dish. Shape mixture so there is room on all sides. Bake 1 hour at 325°.

title: Quick Russian Tea
description: A warm and fragrant beverage made from a blend of lemonade mix, cinnamon, cloves, and sugar. This quick Russian tea mixture is perfect for a comforting drink on a cold day.
instructions: 1. Mix all ingredients together thoroughly and put into a tightly closed container. Use 2 to 3 heaping teaspoons of mixture for each cup hot water. Will keep up to six months.

title: Reuben Appetizers
description: Savory Reuben-inspired appetizers with party rye bread, corned beef, sauerkraut, Swiss cheese, and dressing, broiled until warm and cheesy. Perfect for a quick snack or party starter.
instructions:
1. Place bread slices on cookie sheet.
2. Add corned beef, sauerkraut, dressing and cheese.
3. Broil about 6 inches away from heat for 2 to 3 minutes or until cheese melts.
4. Garnish with olive slice or tomato slice.
5. Serves 30.

title: Quick Borsch
description: A quick and refreshing chilled borscht, blending beets with consommé, celery soup, and horseradish. This vibrant soup is perfect for a light and flavorful meal, garnished with sour cream.
instructions:
1. Place beets and half the beet liquid in blender.
2. Add remaining ingredients and blend until smooth.
3. Chill at least 3 hours.
4. Serve garnished with a dollop of sour cream and a pinch of chopped tarragon or parsley.
5. Serves 6.

title: Jean'S Mess
description: A hearty one-pot meal featuring browned ground beef, onion, and garlic simmered in a creamy blend of condensed soups. This comforting dish is simple to prepare for a satisfying dinner.
instructions:
1. In large pot, brown meat, onion and garlic.
2. Drain excess fat.
3. Add soups, one at a time (no water or milk).
4. Cook on low heat for 45 minutes, or until bubbly.
5. Stir occasionally to keep from sticking. Serve over Chinese noodles, egg noodles, or rice.

title: Jimmy Dean Breakfast Burrito
description: Flavorful breakfast burritos filled with seasoned sausage, onions, potatoes, and scrambled eggs, all wrapped in a warm flour tortilla. A delicious and satisfying way to start your day.
instructions:
1. Brown sausage and onions in skillet.
2. Drain.
3. Add diced potato and eggs and scramble all together.
4. Place generous amount of cooked mixture in a steamed tortilla.
5. Excellent served with picante sauce.

title: Reuben Rolls
description: Savory Reuben-inspired rolls with a delicious filling of corned beef, Swiss cheese, and sauerkraut, enveloped in pizza dough and baked until golden. A fun and easy dish for snacking or a light meal.
instructions:
1. Blend corn beef, cheese and sauerkraut together.
2. Add Miracle Whip and mustard.
3. Roll out pizza dough and spread mixture all over.
4. Roll dough up and bake on cookie sheet at 425° for 10 minutes or 350° for 25 minutes.

title: Cheese Potato Dish
description: A comforting potato casserole combining frozen hash browns, creamy chicken soup, Cheddar cheese, and onion, baked until bubbly and topped with a buttery, crispy crust. A perfect side dish.
instructions:
1. Mix it all up except margarine.
2. Put in a 13 x 9 x 2-inch pan. Dribble butter over the top.
3. Top with crushed potato chips or corn flakes.
4. Bake 1 hour at 350°.

title: German Potato Pancakes (Kartoffelpuffer)
description: Traditional German Kartoffelpuffer, crisp potato pancakes seasoned with onion, egg, and nutmeg, perfect for a savory snack or side dish. These golden delights are pan-fried until perfection.
instructions:
1. Peel and shred (or grate) potatoes and onion.
2. Place them in a large bowl.
3. In a small bowl, beat the egg.
4. Gradually add the flour, salt, parsley and nutmeg, beating with a wire whisk.
5. Pour the mixture over the shredded potatoes and onion, stirring to combine.

title: Clam Mushroom Salad
description: A refreshing clam and mushroom salad with fresh lettuce, red onion, and tomato, dressed in a tangy clam juice vinaigrette. A light and flavorful salad for any meal.
instructions:
1. Core, rinse and drain lettuce.
2. Chill in disposable plastic bag or plastic crisper.
3. Clean mushrooms with damp paper towels; trim stems.
4. Slice lengthwise.
5. Combine clam juice, oil, vinegar, salt, tarragon and Tabasco in large jar or juice container; cover and shake well.
6. Add mushrooms and onion.
7. Shake to coat.
8. Chill.
9. Tear enough lettuce to make 5 cups.
10. Chill remainder for use another time.
11. Cut tomato into wedges.
12. Combine with lettuce and mushroom mixture in large salad bowl; toss lightly.
13. Add fresh clams to taste.
14. Makes 5 to 6 servings.

title: Polish Sausage And Lentils
description: Hearty and flavorful combination of Polish sausage and tender lentils, simmered with onions, garlic, tomatoes, and bay leaf. A comforting and satisfying meal perfect for a chilly evening.
instructions:
1. Rinse lentils.
2. Put in large saucepan with salted water to cover and cook over low heat for about 20 minutes or until tender and still holding shape.
3. Drain and reserve liquid.

title: Sweet And Sour Kielbasa Casserole
description: A delightful sweet and sour kielbasa casserole featuring sliced kielbasa, butter beans, and an tangy sauce with tomato soup, brown sugar, and ketchup. Baked until bubbly and flavorful.
instructions:
1. Cut kielbasa into 1/4-inch slices.
2. Mix remainder of ingredients together.
3. Pour mixture first into 2-liter size casserole dish.
4. Layer kielbasa on top in carpet-like fashion. Bake 1 hour at 350°, uncovered.

title: Kiddie Chow
description: A delicious kiddie chow recipe.
instructions:
1. Melt chocolate chips and butter.
2. Stir in peanut butter.
3. Mix in Chex.
4. Pat into pan.
5. When hardened, sprinkle with powdered sugar.

title: Coconut Bars
description: A delicious coconut bars recipe.
instructions:
1. Beat the first 3 ingredients and bake 20 minutes.
2. Then mix the remaining ingredients except coconut.
3. Spread this over baked batter.
4. Sprinkle with coconut.
5. Bake at 325° for 25 minutes. Cool; cut in bars.

title: Jiffy Stroganoff
description: A delicious jiffy stroganoff recipe.
instructions:
1. Brown meat, vegetables and seasoning.
2. Make sauce of soup and sour cream.
3. Stir into meat mixture and heat through.
4. Serve over hot noodles.

title: Holiday Fondue Pot Or Microwave
description: A delicious holiday fondue pot or microwave recipe.
instructions:
1. Place all ingredients in fondue pot and heat.
2. Or, to microwave, toss cheeses, flour, mustard and garlic powder together. In a 1 1/2-quart microwave casserole dish, heat wine, Worcestershire sauce and 1/4 cup water, uncovered, on 100% power (High) for 1 to 1 1/2 minutes, or until hot.
3. Add cheese mixture to liquid.
4. Microwave at 50% (Medium) for 7 to 9 minutes, or until cheese is melted, stirring every 2 minutes.
5. Serve with vegetables or bread cubes.
6. Reheat as needed.
7. Serves 8.

title: Tortellini In White Sauce
description: A delicious tortellini in white sauce recipe.
instructions:
1. Saute Prosciutto in butter.
2. Add milk; bring to slow boil. Add flour, salt and pepper.
3. Cook on low until it begins to thicken.
4. Add heavy cream and continue to cook on low for 8 minutes.

title: Tortoni Squares
description: A delicious tortoni squares recipe.
instructions:
1. Combine almonds, oleo, crumbs and extract; mix well.
2. Sprinkle 1/3 of crumbs over bottom of 8 1/2 x 11-inch pan that is lined with foil.
3. Spoon half of ice cream over crumbs.
4. Drizzle with preserves.
5. Sprinkle with crumbs.
6. Repeat layers.
7. Store in freezer.
8. Cut in squares and garnish with whipped cream, if desired.

title: Stroganoff Style Pork Chops
description: A delicious stroganoff style pork chops recipe.
instructions:
1. In a large skillet on medium heat brown pork in oil for 5 minutes.
2. Remove; keep warm.
3. In the drippings saute onion and mushrooms until tender, stir in water, mustard and salt; bring to boil.
4. Return chops to pan; reduce heat, cover and simmer for 15 minutes, until chops are tender.
5. Remove and keep warm.
6. Combine flour and sour cream until smooth; add to skillet.
7. Bring to a boil; cook and stir 1-2 minutes or until thicken.
8. Serve pork chops and mushroom sauce with noodles.

title: Pork Chop Plus
description: A delicious pork chop plus recipe.
instructions:
1. Slice potatoes and onions thin.
2. Place pork chops in bottom of large casserole pan.
3. Place sliced potatoes and onions over pork chops.
4. Add garlic powder, salt and pepper.
5. Cover all ingredients with tomato juice.
6. Cover and bake at 350° until potatoes are tender, about 1 1/2 hours.
7. Serves 6.

title: Chocolate Sin Cake
description: A delicious chocolate sin cake recipe.
instructions:
1. Toss chocolate chips and pecans in 1 tablespoon cake mix. Beat together remaining ingredients, except topping, for 3 minutes, then fold in chocolate chips/pecan mixture.
2. Pour into greased, floured Bundt or tube pan.
3. Bake at 350° for 50 minutes. Cool on wire rack 10 to 15 minutes.
4. Place cake on cake plate. Heat topping until warm (not hot) and fluid.
5. Pour over cake. Serve warm.

title: Blueberry Torte
description: A delicious blueberry torte recipe.
instructions: 1. Mix graham cracker crumbs, 1/2 cup sugar and melted oleo. Press into a 9 x 13-inch pan.

title: Kona Banana Bread
description: A delicious kona banana bread recipe.
instructions:
1. Cream shortening and sugar, then add eggs.
2. Stir in bananas. Add flour and mix well.
3. Pour in 9 x 9 x 2-inch pan and bake at 350° for 30 to 35 minutes.

title: Stuffed Cabbage In Tomato Sauce
description: A delicious stuffed cabbage in tomato sauce recipe.
instructions:
1. Mix the ground beef, rice, onion, salt, pepper and egg until well blended.
2. Trim off thickest part of stem from cabbage leaves. Divide meat mixture into 12 portions.
3. Wrap each in a leaf, fastening with toothpicks.
4. Brown cabbage rolls in oil.
5. Add contents of 2 cans of tomato sauce and 1/4 cup water.
6. Cover; simmer for 40 minutes.
7. Makes 6 servings at 315 calories per serving.

title: Beef And Sausage Hors D'Oeuvre
description: A delicious beef and sausage hors d'oeuvre recipe.
instructions: 1. You will need aluminum foil and a cookie sheet.

title: Baline (Potato Pancake)
description: A delicious baline (potato pancake) recipe.
instructions:
1. Put potatoes and onion through a blender.
2. Add the egg, baking powder and flour (as needed to hold other ingredients together). Shape into patties and fry in fat (just so covered) until brown. These may also be stuffed with cheese for a variety.

title: Party Spinach Dip(Low-Fat)
description: A delicious party spinach dip(low-fat) recipe.
instructions:
1. Process cottage cheese in a blender until smooth.
2. Press spinach between paper towels until almost dry.
3. Put cottage cheese, spinach and remaining ingredients into a bowl.
4. Blend well, folding ingredients together.
5. Cover and refrigerate 3 hours.
6. Yields 3 cups.
7. Has 14 calories and a trace of fat per tablespoon.

title: Polish Meatballs
description: A delicious polish meatballs recipe.
instructions:
1. Mix first 4 ingredients and form into very small balls.
2. Place in oblong pan.
3. Mix remaining ingredients and pour over meatballs. Bake for 1 1/2 to 2 hours in preheated 350° oven.
4. Cover them for the first hour of baking.

title: Mexican Stack Ups
description: A delicious mexican stack ups recipe.
instructions:
1. Brown hamburger, drain; mix with taco seasoning as directed on package.
2. Heat refried beans on low in medium pan.
3. Microwave tortillas 3 to 4 minutes in package, turning once.
4. Place tortilla on plate.
5. Spread tablespoon of beans and tablespoon of meat on it. Add cheese, lettuce, tomatoes and onions.
6. Place second tortilla on top and repeat steps (beans, meat, cheese, lettuce, tomatoes and onions).
7. Serves 6.

title: Mexican Blintzes
description: A delicious mexican blintzes recipe.
instructions:
1. Cut Monterey Jack cheese into 3 x 1 1/4-inch sliess.
2. Cut chilies into strips.
3. Heat tortilla until warm; place one slice of cheese in center.
4. Place chili strip on cheese.
5. Fold over ends and roll up; secure with toothpick.
6. Repeat with each tortilla. Deep fry blintzes at 400° a few at a time, until golden brown. Drain on paper towels and remove toothpicks.
7. Cut in half crosswise and stand on end to prevent cheese from running.
8. Serve with dip.
9. Makes 24.

title: Easy French Onion Rice
description: A delicious easy french onion rice recipe.
instructions:
1. Saute garlic in butter.
2. Add rice and mix together.
3. Add soup, water, and mushrooms with juice.
4. Pepper to taste.
5. Stir and bring to a boil.
6. Stir again. Cover, reduce heat, and simmer for 20 mins. or until liquid is absorbed.

title: Sugar Cream Pie(My Mother-In-Law'S Recipe)
description: A delicious sugar cream pie(my mother-in-law's recipe) recipe.
instructions:
1. Mix together in pan.
2. A little at a time, add 1 pint of half and half.
3. Add butter the size of a walnut.
4. Bring to boil.
5. Cook until thick.
6. Cool slightly.
7. Pour in unbaked pie shell.
8. Bake at 350° until brown.

title: Skillet Pork And Sauerkraut
description: A delicious skillet pork and sauerkraut recipe.
instructions:
1. Broil chops on preheated broiler 4 inches from heat 2 minutes on each side.
2. Heat oil in large skillet.
3. Add onion and garlic. Cook over medium heat until softened.
4. Add 1/4 cup water and remaining ingredients except apple.
5. Top with chops and simmer, covered, on low heat about 1 hour or until chops are tender.
6. Then stir in apple slices and simmer about 3 minutes.
7. Serves 2.

title: Crisp Onion-Roasted Potatoes
description: A delicious crisp onion-roasted potatoes recipe.
instructions:
1. Preheat oven to 450°.
2. In shallow baking pan, thoroughly blend all ingredients except potatoes.
3. (It is possible to do these with less oil and margarine than the recipe calls for.
4. I use only enough to coat the bottom of the pan I'm using.)
5. Add potatoes and turn to coat.
6. Bake, turning occasionally, 60 minutes (usually less) or until potatoes are tender and golden brown.
7. Garnish, if desired, with parsley.
8. Makes about 8 servings.

title: Tuna Stroganoff
description: A delicious tuna stroganoff recipe.
instructions:
1. Dip dove breasts in soy sauce.
2. Wrap with 1/2 slice of bacon and secure with a toothpick if necessary. Place on grill or in the oven (350°) on a rack in a pan.
3. Cook for approximately 30 minutes or until the breast is tender.

title: Sauerkraut And Meat
description: A delicious sauerkraut and meat recipe.
instructions:
1. Cook bacon until brown, drain.
2. Saute onion in bacon drippings.
3. Add kraut and bacon mix. Put in 9 x 13-inch pan.
4. Heat water, dissolve sugar, bouillon and caraway seed.
5. Pour over kraut.
6. Add meat.
7. Bake at 350° for 1 1/2 hours, covered.

title: Orange Mushroom Salad
description: A delicious orange mushroom salad recipe.
instructions:
1. Toss lettuce, orange slices, mushrooms and onions with dressing until well coated.
2. Serve on lettuce leaves.
3. Top with raisins, if desired.

title: Potato Stuffing Casserole
description: A delicious potato stuffing casserole recipe.
instructions:
1. In a medium skillet, saute celery and onion in 2 tablespoons butter until tender.
2. Add bread cubes and stir until lightly browned.
3. Stir in potatoes, parsley, salt, pepper, milk and egg. Mix well.
4. Spoon into a greased 1 1/2-quart baking dish.
5. Dot with remaining butter.
6. Bake at 350°, uncovered, for 30 to 40 minutes or until lightly browned.
7. Garnish with additional parsley. Yield:
8. 6 to 8 servings.

title: Tosca Torte(Finland-Sweden)
description: A delicious tosca torte(finland-sweden) recipe.
instructions:
1. Preheat oven to 325°.
2. Beat eggs and sugar until the mixture is light and foamy.
3. Sift flour with baking powder and add to egg mixture.
4. Melt butter over low heat and let cool.
5. Add cream and melted butter to batter.
6. Butter and flour a round cake form or frying pan with high boards.
7. Pour butter into pan and cook until it is half done, about 20 minutes.

title: Chicken Milano(An Easy Make-Ahead Casserole)
description: A delicious chicken milano(an easy make-ahead casserole) recipe.
instructions:
1. Saute chicken, onion, celery and pepper in 2 tablespoons oil for about 4 minutes.
2. Add mushrooms and seasonings and simmer 20 minutes.
3. Blend in soup and milk and cook 5 minutes.
4. Cook linguine according to package directions and toss with chicken mixture.
5. Pour into a 2-quart casserole.
6. Overlap tomato slices on top.
7. Toss croutons with butter and spread over tomatoes.
8. Bake 20 minutes at 350°
9. for 20 minutes.
10. Top with cheese and bake another 10 minutes.

title: Brownie Chocolate Cake
description: A delicious brownie chocolate cake recipe.
instructions:
1. Sift sugar, flour and salt into a bowl.
2. In a saucepan, place butter, cocoa and water.
3. Bring to a boil.
4. Stir into sifted dry ingredients; mix well.
5. In
6. a separate bowl, beat eggs.
7. Add vanilla, soda and buttermilk.
8. Stir into first mixture.
9. Pour into greased sheet cake pan.
10. Bake at 375° for 20 minutes or until cake tests done.

title: *Stuffed Cabbage With Lemon Sauce
description: A delicious *stuffed cabbage with lemon sauce recipe.
instructions:
1. Place cabbage in pot filled with boiling water. Boil for 10 minutes. Separate leaves. Combine meat, rice, onion, salt, pepper and parsley.
2. Place about 2 tablespoons of filling in each cabbage leaf.
3. Loosely fold over sides and roll.
4. Place rolls into large pot, keeping them tight.
5. Dot with the butter and pour the 3 cups of hot water into pot.
6. Bring to a boil and cook over low heat for about 1 to 1 1/2 hours.

title: Roasted Carrot And Beet Soup
description: A delicious roasted carrot and beet soup recipe.
instructions:
1. Preheat oven to 350 degrees.
2. Place carrots, celery and onion in a shallow oven proof dish.
3. Dot with butter and sprinkle with the sugar, ginger and mace.
4. Pour 2 c. of broth into the bottom of the dish.
5. Cover tightly with aluminum foil.
6. Bake for 2 hours. Remove vegetables from the oven and transfer them along with any liquid, to a large pot.
7. Add 4 c. of broth.
8. Bring to a boil, reduce heat to medium and simmer for 10 minutes partially covered. Cool slightly.
9. Puree the soup along with the cooked beets in a blender or a food processor until smooth.
10. Return to the pot. Season with salt and pepper.
11. Add some of remaining broth for thinner soup, if desired.
12. Garnish with sour cream and chives.

title: Enchiladas(Joe And Sonya Kuykendall, 11-3-78)
description: A delicious enchiladas(joe and sonya kuykendall, 11-3-78) recipe.
instructions:
1. Put can of tomato sauce and enchilada mix in pan and mix. Heat over low fire.
2. Brown beef and onion over low fire in skillet.
3. After sauce heats 10 minutes, add to the beef, just enough to be slightly soupy (to your preference).

title: Chicken  Swirl Slices
description: A delicious chicken  swirl slices recipe.
instructions:
1. Heat oven to 350°.
2. Place 1 chicken breast, boned side up between 2 pieces of plastic wrap or waxed paper.
3. Working from center, gently pound chicken with rolling pin or flat side of meat mallet until about 1/8 to 1/4 inch thick.
4. Repeat with remaining chicken breast halves.
5. In a small bowl combine marjoram, thyme and rosemary.
6. Brush flattened chicken with melted butter; sprinkle each piece with herbs.
7. Place ham slice on each chicken piece; top with cheese slice.

title: Swedish Rye Bread(Bread Machine Recipe)
description: A delicious swedish rye bread(bread machine recipe) recipe.
instructions:
1. Mix dry ingredients, except yeast and set aside.
2. Pour water and molasses into bread machine.
3. Add dry ingredients, yeast and butter.
4. Set machine for dough cycle. During the knead cycle, add any additional water or flour to bring dough to a soft (not gummy) consistency.
5. At end of dough cycle, turn onto floured surface. Let rest for 15 minutes.
6. Form into a round loaf.
7. Place onto a lightly greased baking sheet that has been sprinkled with corn meal.
8. Cover with a clean dish towel and let rise until doubled. Preheat oven to 375°.
9. Bake for 30 to 35 minutes.
10. Loaf should make a hollow sound when tapped on the bottom.
11. Cool for 15 minutes before slicing.
12. Serve buttered with paper-thin sliced ham for a special treat.

title: Polish Easter Cake
description: A delicious polish easter cake recipe.
instructions:
1. Scald milk.
2. Stir in sugar, salt and butter.
3. Cool to lukewarm.
4. Pour lukewarm water into a large bowl. Sprinkle yeast over top and stir until dissolved.
5. Add milk, eggs and flour. Beat vigorously for 5 minutes.
6. Cover and let rise in a warm place for 1 1/2 hours or until doubled.
7. Stir down batter.
8. Beat in almonds, raisins and lemon peel.
9. Pour batter into greased and floured Charlotte mold or deep cake pan.
10. Let rise for 1 hour. Bake at 350° for 50 minutes.
11. Let cool in pan for 20 minutes, then remove.

title: Clainy'S Frogmore Stew
description: A delicious clainy's frogmore stew recipe.
instructions:
1. Fill large Dutch oven halfway with water; add 1/4 cup Old Bay seasoning.
2. Bring to a boil; add potatoes and bring to a second boil.
3. Add sausage and corn and bring to boil.
4. Cook 10 minutes or until potatoes are tender.
5. Add shrimp, pepper and juice and cook 3 to 5 minutes or until shrimp turns pink.
6. Remove with slotted spoon onto a serving platter.
7. Serve immediately with cocktail sauce.

title: Microwave Chex Party Mix
description: A quick and easy savory snack mix, perfect for parties or anytime munching. This recipe features cereals, nuts, and pretzels coated in a buttery, seasoned sauce and baked until crispy.
instructions: 1. Melt butter on High for 30 seconds. Add salt and Worcestershire sauce; mix well. Pour cereals, nuts and pretzels in a large plastic bag. Pour butter mixture over cereal; shake to coat. Pour into a large bowl and microwave on High for 3 minutes, stirring every minute. Spread on paper towels to cool.

title: Mushroom Sauce(Served On Christmas Eve Over Varenyky-Pyrohy)
description: A rich and savory mushroom sauce traditionally served over varenyky (Ukrainian dumplings). This dish brings earthy flavors to your holiday table, especially on Christmas Eve.
instructions:
1. Rinse dried mushrooms and soak about 5 to 10 minutes in enough water to cover.
2. Chop mushrooms and put in 1 quart saucepan.
3. Add cold water to cover, about 2 to 3 inches from the top of the mushrooms.
4. Cover; bring to a boil, reduce heat to simmer and add fresh chopped mushrooms.
5. Cook about 1 hour until soft.

title: Quick Hearty Nacho Dip
description: This quick and hearty nacho dip combines seasoned ground beef, beans, and picante sauce, topped with melted Cheddar cheese. It's a perfect appetizer for gatherings, served hot with tortilla chips.
instructions:
1. Cook first 3 ingredients in large skillet until meat is brown; drain.
2. Add beans, picante sauce and taco seasoning mix. Stir well.
3. Cook over medium heat until blended.
4. Spoon into 12 x 8 x 2 dish.
5. Sprinkle with cheese.
6. Bake at 400° for 5 minutes until cheese melts.
7. Serve with tortilla chips.

title: Ugly Shonton Meat Balls
description: Despite their name, these meat balls are delicious! Made with ground chuck and simmered in a creamy mushroom and wine sauce, they are a comforting meal option.
instructions:
1. Mix first 7 ingredients well and form into balls.
2. Brown gently, add wine.
3. Simmer until wine is evaporated.
4. Add soup mixed with milk.
5. Simmer until sauce is down to about half.

title: Dorothy'S Old Fashioned Saltwater Dills
description: A classic recipe for old-fashioned saltwater dill pickles. Cucumbers are brined with garlic, dill, and optional hot peppers for a crisp and tangy treat.
instructions: 1. Bring to boil. Place cucumbers in sterilized jars, wide-mouth if possible. In each jar, place a clove of fresh garlic, hot pepper, if desired, a fresh grape leaf and fresh green dill. Pour the boiling brine over the cucumbers. Seal tightly and store about 30 days.

title: Herbed Mushroom Salad
description: A refreshing and savory mushroom salad marinated in a blend of herbs, lemon, and Worcestershire sauce. Serve over a bed of shredded lettuce for a light appetizer or side dish.
instructions:
1. Place mushrooms in small bowl.
2. Combine all ingredients except shredded lettuce and pour over mushrooms.
3. Marinate a few hours or overnight.
4. Arrange shredded lettuce in salad bowls; top with mushrooms.

title: Quickie Meat And Cheese Dip
description: An incredibly simple and fast cheese and chili dip, perfect for a quick snack. Just microwave Velveeta cheese and chili, then serve with chips.
instructions: 1. "Nuke" in microwave and eat with chips.

title: Fasta Pasta - 10 Minute Pasta Sauce
description: A quick and vibrant pasta sauce featuring fresh tomatoes, basil, garlic, and a hint of red wine. This sauce comes together in just 10 minutes, perfect for a fast meal.
instructions:
1. In a heavy skillet, add oil, fresh tomatoes and 1/4 of wine. Saute until the tomatoes cook down until soft and tender.
2. Add garlic, onion, basil and can of cooked tomatoes and rest of wine. Cook on high for 2 minutes, then cover and simmer for 10 minutes. Pour over your choice of pasta and add Parmesan cheese.

title: Party Cucumber Sandwiches
description: Elegant and easy party cucumber sandwiches, perfect for appetizers or tea time. Cream cheese and mayonnaise are flavored with Italian dressing mix and spread on rye bread, topped with fresh cucumber slices.
instructions:
1. Mix first 3 ingredients and spread on bread.
2. Cut in small squares and top with cucumber slices.

title: *Low-Cholesterol Pound Cake*
description: A moist and tender low-cholesterol pound cake, perfect for dessert or a snack. This recipe uses margarine and egg substitutes to create a lighter version of a classic.
instructions:
1. Cream the margarine, shortening and sugar in mixer bowl until light and fluffy.
2. Add egg white; beat well.
3. Add egg substitute; beat well.
4. Add flour alternately with milk, beating well after each addition.
5. Add flavorings.
6. Pour into greased and floured tube pan.
7. Bake at 350° for 1 hour and 15 minutes.

title: Potato Pancake
description: A simple yet satisfying potato pancake, crispy on the outside and tender within. Potatoes are pan-fried until golden, seasoned with salt and pepper.
instructions:
1. Put 1 tablespoon oil and 1 tablespoon butter in a large skillet.
2. Set over medium heat until butter melts.
3. Put potatoes in the skillet.
4. Press them down into an even compact layer. Cook, covered, for 5 minutes.
5. Uncover, turn heat to low and cook for 15 minutes more.
6. Slide the pancake onto a plate.
7. Put 1 tablespoon oil and 1 tablespoon butter into the skillet.
8. When the butter melts, put the pancake back into the skillet with the browned side up.
9. Press down to compact the potatoes and cook, uncovered, for 15 minutes more. Cut into 4 wedges and serve warm.

title: Hamburger Barbecue(Sloppy Joes)
description: Classic Hamburger Barbecue, often known as Sloppy Joes, featuring seasoned ground beef simmered in a tangy tomato-based sauce. Perfect served on buns for a family-friendly meal.
instructions:
1. Melt margarine in large skillet.
2. Brown hamburger meat.
3. Mix onions, green pepper and celery.
4. Cook over low heat 5 minutes. Blend in tomato sauce, chili sauce, Worcestershire, pepper, salt, sugar and vinegar.
5. Simmer for about 15 minutes, stirring often. Serve on hamburger buns.

title: Heavenly Ham Salad
description: A refreshing ham salad featuring shell macaroni, crisp vegetables, and a creamy yogurt-based dressing. This dish is perfect for picnics or a light lunch.
instructions:
1. Prepare and cook macaroni; set aside.
2. Drain.
3. Combine macaroni, ham, celery, radishes and cucumber.
