4. Blend together mayonnaise, yogurt, mustard and garlic salt.
5. Toss dressing with salad mixture.
6. Serve immediately or cover and chill.
7. Refrigerate leftovers.
8. Serves 6 to 8.

title: English Pasty
description: A hearty English Pasty, filled with layers of diced potatoes, minced onion, and tender pork loin, all encased in a savory pie crust. Perfect for a comforting meal.
instructions:
1. Place bottom crust in 8-inch pie pan.
2. Spread diced potatoes evenly over bottom crust.
3. Sprinkle minced onion over potatoes. Spread pieces of pork evenly on top of potatoes and onion.
4. Season to taste.
5. Cover with vented top pie crust and bake at 350° for 1 hour.

title: Breakfast Blintz Souffle
description: A sweet and comforting breakfast blintz souffle featuring frozen cheese blintzes baked in a rich custard. This dish is perfect for a special breakfast or brunch.
instructions:
1. Melt butter in a 13 x 9-inch pan.
2. Put frozen blintzes in dish and coat well with butter.
3. Mix remaining ingredients with electric mixer and pour over blintzes.
4. Be sure to cover all. Bake 50 minutes at 350°.
5. Serve with applesauce, sour cream or jam.

title: American Pierogi Casserole
description: An 'Americanized' take on pierogi in casserole form, featuring layers of noodles, mashed potato and onion filling, and American cheese. It's a comforting and hearty dish suitable for families.
instructions:
1. Saute onion in butter and
2. add
3. to
4. potatoes which have been boiled and drained.
5. Mash
6. together.
7. Butter large casserole dish and add 1/2 of
8. the
9. noodles.
10. Spread
11. mashed potato mixture over noodles and top with
12. slices of cheese.
13. Add rest of noodles and again top with cheese slices.
14. Dot with butter; cover.
15. Bake in 350° oven until cheese melts.

title: Pan Pierogi(This Is A Lazy Way To Make Pierogi!)
description: This is a 'lazy' way to enjoy the flavors of pierogi without all the traditional effort, using lasagna noodles filled with a creamy potato and Velveeta cheese mash. Pan-fried onions complete this comforting dish.
instructions:
1. Boil potatoes in salted water; mash with butter, milk and Velveeta cheese.
2. (This can be done the night before.) Cook lasagna noodles.
3. While noodles are cooking, fry onions in butter. Set aside.
4. Butter a large casserole dish (9 x 13-inch).
5. Layer four noodles on bottom of pan and spread half of the potato mixture on them.
6. Top with 4 more noodles.
7. Put half the onions on top of noodles and spread the rest of the potato mixture.
8. Place the last 4 noodles on top and cover with onions.
9. Bake at 325° for 45 minutes.
10. Let stand about 10 minutes before serving.

title: Polish Beet Soup
description: A classic Polish beet soup, rich in flavor with tender spareribs, bay leaf, and a delightful tang from vinegar. This vibrant red soup is a hearty and traditional dish.
instructions:
1. Drain beet juice into a glass and add 2 tablespoons vinegar and 1 teaspoon salt.
2. Cut beets in thin strips and place in a bowl with 3 tablespoons vinegar and 1 teaspoon salt.
3. Set aside.

title: Stella Adamek'S Rye Bread
description: Homemade rye bread combining different flours for a rich texture. It involves proofing, kneading, and baking, perfect for a hearty sandwich or alongside a warm meal.
instructions:
1. Combine first 5 ingredients in large bowl.
2. Combine next 3 ingredients in a cup and add to dry ingredients.
3. Work in 9 cups white flour.
4. Place on floured board and knead for 8 to 10 minutes.
5. Place in greased container and let raise to double the size.

title: Trish'S Polska Kielbasa Soup
description: A comforting Polish kielbasa soup, enriched with chicken broth and hearty vegetables like carrots, cabbage, and broccoli. Quick to thicken with instant mashed potatoes for a satisfying meal.
instructions:
1. Combine chicken broth, water, Polska kielbasa and carrots. Simmer for one hour, then add cabbage and broccoli.
2. Simmer until tender, then add instant potatoes to desired thickness.
3. Pepper to taste.
4. Knorr all-purpose seasoning to taste.

title: Stuffed Hot Peppers(Canning Recipe)
description: A canning recipe for stuffed hot peppers filled with sauerkraut and garlic, preserved in a vinegar brine. Ideal for a spicy and tangy condiment to enjoy year-round.
instructions:
1. Core peppers (don't break), wearing rubber gloves.
2. Wash and boil 2 minutes.
3. Cool in ice water.
4. Stuff with sauerkraut.
5. Pack in sterilized jars.
6. Add garlic and alum.
7. Cover with solution of water, vinegar and salt.
8. Boil in hot bath for 3 to 5 minutes. Seal.

title: San Jose Quiche
description: A savory San Jose Quiche featuring ground beef, onions, garlic, and green peppers, seasoned with Mexican spices. Layered with tortillas and cheese, this is a hearty and flavorful main dish.
instructions:
1. Brown beef; add chopped onions, garlic and green pepper.
2. Cook 5 minutes.
3. Stir in salsa, oregano, cumin, chili powder and salt. Grease casserole dish and put a layer of tortilla chips in dish. Top with meat mixture.
4. Spread on chile Ortegas, and top with cheese.
5. Beat eggs with milk and 1/2 teaspoon salt.
6. Pour over top.
7. Bake at 375° for 25 to 30 minutes or until set.
8. Garnish with olives.

title: Fried Fish Batter
description: A simple and quick batter for perfectly fried fish fillets. Using pancake mix, it creates a crispy coating in just minutes.
instructions:
1. Mix first three ingredients.
2. Coat fish with pancake mix.
3. Dip fillets in batter.
4. Fry 4 to 6 minutes.

title: Russian Cole Slaw
description: A refreshing Russian-style coleslaw with finely chopped cabbage and green onions, lightly sweetened with sugar and tangy vinegar. A simple side dish that complements any meal.
instructions:
1. Chop cabbage and onions finely.
2. Add sugar.

title: Campfire Goulash
description: A hearty campfire goulash featuring sweet sausage, onions, potatoes, and tomatoes. This one-pot meal is perfect for outdoor cooking or a comforting weeknight dinner, simmering to rich perfection.
instructions:
1. In a large saucepan or Dutch oven, saute sausage until lightly browned on both sides.
2. Add onion and saute until transparent. Add potatoes and sauce mix. Gradually stir in water.
3. Bring to a boil and cover. Simmer for 15 minutes, stirring often.
4. Add tomatoes and green pepper. Mix well.
5. Cover and simmer for 10 minutes longer.
6. Yields 6 servings.

title: Stew Beef Casserole
description: A simple and comforting stew beef casserole, slow-baked with a blend of savory soups and sauces. This easy-to-prepare dish yields tender beef and a rich flavor, perfect for a cozy dinner.
instructions:
1. Mix well together the soup, A.1. sauce and Worcestershire sauce.
2. Add to stew beef which has been salted and peppered to taste.
3. Put in casserole dish.
4. Bake, covered, for 3 hours at 300°.
5. Serve over rice.

title: Challah
description: A traditional Challaha, a rich and slightly sweet bread, characterized by its beautiful braided shape. Made with yeast, eggs, and honey, it's perfect for holidays or a special breakfast.
instructions:
1. Dissolve yeast in warm water with 2 tablespoons sugar.
2. Cover. Let stand in warm place.
3. Place butter in mixing bowl with salt and boiling water.
4. When melted, add oil, sugar and honey.
5. Add 6 eggs, one at a time.
6. Mix at low speed.
7. Add yeast, mix 4 cups flour.
8. Mix well.
9. Add remaining flour and knead 10 minutes on table dusted with flour.
10. Add flour to knead.
11. Put in buttered bowl and turn, butter side up.
12. Cover, let rise 1 hour, until doubled.
13. Punch down.
14. Let rise again 1 hour until doubled.

title: Swiss Potato Pancakes
description: Crispy Swiss potato pancakes, enhanced with Swiss cheese and a hint of onions. Pan-fried until golden, these savory pancakes are perfect as a side dish or light meal.
instructions:
1. Measure flour, sugar and salt into large bowl.
2. Mix well with fork.
3. Add shortening, mix with fork until crumbly.
4. In small bowl beat together egg, water and vinegar (using fork to blend well). Combine dry ingredients with liquid.
5. Divide dough into 5 equal portions and shape into round flattened patties, ready for rolling. Wrap each patty in plastic wrap, chill 1/2 hour before using.
6. When ready to roll, lightly flour both sides of patty and roll out on lightly floured board.
7. Makes 5 (9-inch) pie crusts or 20 tart shells.
8. Can be frozen after shaping into round flattened patties.

title: Crock-Pot Kielbasa
description: Slow-cooked kielbasa simmered in a tangy-sweet sauce of ketchup, mustard, vinegar, and brown sugar. Perfect for a cozy meal, as an appetizer, or in a bun.
instructions:
1. Boil kielbasa in beer for 10 minutes.
2. Combine ketchup, mustard, vinegar and sugar in crock-pot.
3. Cut kielbasa in small bite size pieces and place in crock-pot.
4. Cook on high for 2 to 3 hours or low for 3 to 5 hours.
5. Enjoy.
